scope: TorchTitan MORI+AITER routed backward AsyncLL staged dX/dTopk route-key alignment gate

torchtitan_mori_aiter_moe.change: Added route-key reorder using (src_pos, packed_ids) and improved first-mismatch diagnostics for AsyncLL staged route alignment.

launcher.change: Added CANARY_DEBUG_SEED passthrough to --debug.seed so route-alignment A/Bs can be pinned.

probe.change: Standalone AsyncLL route-order probe retained as route metadata reference.

asyncll_unseeded_routekey_debug.read: Passes one unseeded S4096 step with strict route-key alignment, but this is not a parity proof because the matching sync unseeded reference used different random/data state.

sync_unseeded_reference.read: Confirms the retained sync path is healthy, but loss/grad differ from the unseeded AsyncLL run, so the pair is not a deterministic parity gate.

asyncll_seeded_routekey.read: The learned-router long-sequence AsyncLL split-handle path is not route-key safe. The saved x dispatch metadata has zero packed ids for early route keys, while grad/topk dispatch metadata has real top-k ids.

sync_seeded_reference.read: The retained sync registered-combine path passes under the same fixed seed, local VJP, activation-checkpointing, and route settings.

conclusion.read: Simple route-key alignment by (src_pos, packed_ids) is insufficient for integrated learned-router S4096. The current AsyncLL staged dX implementation should stay diagnostic-only; the next overlap attempt needs either a shared MORI route/order handle or explicit dispatched payload/source metadata that gives x, grad_output, and top-k dummy the same route keys.
conclusion.retained_runtime_path: sync registered combine with routed padded VJP
conclusion.next_branch: Build a real route metadata contract for AsyncLL staged dX, then rerun the seeded S4096 parity gate before any throughput A/B.
